metadata.name: World Cup 2026 Full JSON Database
metadata.description: Arquivo JSON único para uso como banco provisório da API da Copa do Mundo.
metadata.importantNotice: Este arquivo é um banco provisório para desenvolvimento. Inclui as 48 seleções, grupos, técnicos, jogadores/slots, campeões históricos, edições da Copa e estádios. Jogadores com status 'provisional_current_squad' são nomes de listas públicas recentes. Jogadores com status 'pending_confirmation' são slots reservados para completar a estrutura sem inventar dados. Os elencos podem mudar até a confirmação oficial e por substituições permitidas. Datas, estádios e horários locais da fase de grupos foram atualizados com base na tabela pública de jogos da Copa do Mundo FIFA 2026.

stadiums.name: Estadio Azteca
stadiums.description: Estadio Azteca, located in Mexico City, is a historic football stadium that has hosted three FIFA World Cups and is set to host the opening match of the 2026 tournament.
stadiums.architect: Pedro Ramírez Vázquez and Rafael Mijares

stadiums.name: Estadio Akron
stadiums.description: Estadio Akron, home to Chivas Guadalajara, is a modern stadium in Zapopan, Mexico, set to host four group-stage matches in the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Host of four World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Estadio BBVA
stadiums.description: Estadio BBVA, also known as "El Gigante de Acero," is a modern football stadium in Guadalupe, Mexico, home to CF Monterrey, with a capacity of 53,500.
highlights: Located near Cerro de la Silla mountain.

stadiums.name: BMO Field
stadiums.description: BMO Field, located in Toronto, Canada, is a soccer-specific stadium that will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Toronto FC and the Canadian national team.
Expanded capacity of 45,736 for the World Cup.
Located on the shores of Lake Ontario at Exhibition Place.
Known as 'Toronto Stadium' during the World Cup.
Will host Canada's opening match against Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 12, 2026.

stadiums.name: BC Place
stadiums.description: BC Place is a multi-purpose stadium in Vancouver, Canada, hosting seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, including Canada's group-stage games.
highlights: Hybrid grass pitch installed for World Cup
Home to Vancouver Whitecaps (MLS) and BC Lions (CFL)

stadiums.name: MetLife Stadium
stadiums.description: MetLife Stadium, located in East Rutherford, NJ, is the home of the New York Giants and New York Jets. It has a capacity of 82,500 and will host the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final.
highlights: Home to New York Giants and Jets
Scheduled to host 2026 FIFA World Cup Final

stadiums.name: SoFi Stadium
stadiums.description: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California, is a state-of-the-art venue hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Los Angeles Rams and Chargers
Scheduled for 2026 FIFA World Cup matches

stadiums.name: AT&T Stadium
stadiums.description: AT&T Stadium, located in Arlington, Texas, is a state-of-the-art venue hosting nine matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, including a semifinal.
highlights: Scheduled to host nine World Cup matches

stadiums.name: NRG Stadium
stadiums.description: NRG Stadium in Houston, Texas, is a retractable-roof stadium hosting NFL games and major events, including seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
highlights: First NFL stadium with retractable roof
Hosts major events like Super Bowls
Renamed 'Houston Stadium' for FIFA World Cup 2026

stadiums.name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
stadiums.description: Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta is a state-of-the-art venue hosting NFL, MLS, and NWSL games, featuring a retractable roof and 75,000-seat capacity.
highlights: Venue for 2026 FIFA World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Hard Rock Stadium
stadiums.description: Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, hosts seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, including a quarter-final and the third-place play-off.
highlights: Home to Miami Dolphins and Miami Hurricanes

stadiums.name: Gillette Stadium
stadiums.description: Gillette Stadium, located in Foxborough, Massachusetts, is set to host FIFA World Cup matches in 2026.
highlights: Home to New England Patriots and Revolution
Renovated in 2023 with new video board and lighthouse
Renamed Boston Stadium for 2026 World Cup

stadiums.name: Lincoln Financial Field
stadiums.description: Lincoln Financial Field, also known as 'The Linc', is a 69,000-seat stadium in Philadelphia, home to the NFL's Eagles and NCAA's Temple Owls. It will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to NFL's Eagles and NCAA's Temple Owls
Hosted 2003 FIFA Women's World Cup matches
Scheduled to host six World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Levi's Stadium
stadiums.description: Levi's Stadium, located in Santa Clara, California, is a modern, sustainable venue hosting six FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.

stadiums.name: Lumen Field
stadiums.description: Lumen Field, located in Seattle, is a multi-purpose stadium known for its electric atmosphere and city skyline views. It will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Seattle Seahawks (NFL) and Seattle Sounders FC (MLS)
Temporarily renamed 'Seattle Stadium' during World Cup
stadiums.architect: Ellerbe Becket, LMN Architects, Streeter & Associates

stadiums.name: Arrowhead Stadium
stadiums.description: Arrowhead Stadium, home to the NFL's Kansas City Chiefs, is set to host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, including a quarterfinal.

stadiums.name: Estadio Azteca
stadiums.description: Estadio Azteca, located in Mexico City, is a historic football stadium that has hosted three FIFA World Cups and is set to host the opening match of the 2026 tournament.
stadiums.architect: Pedro Ramírez Vázquez and Rafael Mijares

stadiums.name: Estadio Akron
stadiums.description: Estadio Akron, home to Chivas Guadalajara, is a modern stadium in Zapopan, Mexico, set to host four group-stage matches in the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Host of four World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Estadio BBVA
stadiums.description: Estadio BBVA, also known as "El Gigante de Acero," is a modern football stadium in Guadalupe, Mexico, home to CF Monterrey, with a capacity of 53,500.
highlights: Located near Cerro de la Silla mountain.

stadiums.name: BMO Field
stadiums.description: BMO Field, located in Toronto, Canada, is a soccer-specific stadium that will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Toronto FC and the Canadian national team.
Expanded capacity of 45,736 for the World Cup.
Located on the shores of Lake Ontario at Exhibition Place.
Known as 'Toronto Stadium' during the World Cup.
Will host Canada's opening match against Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 12, 2026.

stadiums.name: BC Place
stadiums.description: BC Place is a multi-purpose stadium in Vancouver, Canada, hosting seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, including Canada's group-stage games.
highlights: Hybrid grass pitch installed for World Cup
Home to Vancouver Whitecaps (MLS) and BC Lions (CFL)

stadiums.name: MetLife Stadium
stadiums.description: MetLife Stadium, located in East Rutherford, NJ, is the home of the New York Giants and New York Jets. It has a capacity of 82,500 and will host the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final.
highlights: Home to New York Giants and Jets
Scheduled to host 2026 FIFA World Cup Final

stadiums.name: SoFi Stadium
stadiums.description: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California, is a state-of-the-art venue hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Los Angeles Rams and Chargers
Scheduled for 2026 FIFA World Cup matches

stadiums.name: AT&T Stadium
stadiums.description: AT&T Stadium, located in Arlington, Texas, is a state-of-the-art venue hosting nine matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, including a semifinal.
highlights: Scheduled to host nine World Cup matches

stadiums.name: NRG Stadium
stadiums.description: NRG Stadium in Houston, Texas, is a retractable-roof stadium hosting NFL games and major events, including seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
highlights: First NFL stadium with retractable roof
Hosts major events like Super Bowls
Renamed 'Houston Stadium' for FIFA World Cup 2026

stadiums.name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
stadiums.description: Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta is a state-of-the-art venue hosting NFL, MLS, and NWSL games, featuring a retractable roof and 75,000-seat capacity.
highlights: Venue for 2026 FIFA World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Hard Rock Stadium
stadiums.description: Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, hosts seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, including a quarter-final and the third-place play-off.
highlights: Home to Miami Dolphins and Miami Hurricanes

stadiums.name: Gillette Stadium
stadiums.description: Gillette Stadium, located in Foxborough, Massachusetts, is set to host FIFA World Cup matches in 2026.
highlights: Home to New England Patriots and Revolution
Renovated in 2023 with new video board and lighthouse
Renamed Boston Stadium for 2026 World Cup

stadiums.name: Lincoln Financial Field
stadiums.description: Lincoln Financial Field, also known as 'The Linc', is a 69,000-seat stadium in Philadelphia, home to the NFL's Eagles and NCAA's Temple Owls. It will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to NFL's Eagles and NCAA's Temple Owls
Hosted 2003 FIFA Women's World Cup matches
Scheduled to host six World Cup matches

stadiums.name: Levi's Stadium
stadiums.description: Levi's Stadium, located in Santa Clara, California, is a modern, sustainable venue hosting six FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.

stadiums.name: Lumen Field
stadiums.description: Lumen Field, located in Seattle, is a multi-purpose stadium known for its electric atmosphere and city skyline views. It will host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
highlights: Home to Seattle Seahawks (NFL) and Seattle Sounders FC (MLS)
Temporarily renamed 'Seattle Stadium' during World Cup
stadiums.architect: Ellerbe Becket, LMN Architects, Streeter & Associates

stadiums.name: Arrowhead Stadium
stadiums.description: Arrowhead Stadium, home to the NFL's Kansas City Chiefs, is set to host six matches during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, including a quarterfinal.

match-007.summary: No jogo de estreia da Copa do Mundo FIFA 2026, o Canadá empatou em 1 a 1 com a Bósnia e Herzegovina no BMO Field, em Toronto. Jovo Lukic abriu o placar para a Bósnia aos 21 minutos, e Cyle Larin empatou para o Canadá aos 78 minutos.
